answers: Brainstorming rules: no criticism, go for quantity, build on others' ideas, and encourage wild ideas.
Techniques: mind mapping, round-robin, reverse brainstorming (how to make it worse), brainwriting (silent writing).
Tips: set time limit (15-30 min), appoint facilitator, capture ALL ideas, and evaluate after (not during).
Solo brainstorming: write for 10 minutes without stopping, change perspectives, use 'what if' questions.
